title: Almadex Announces Sale of El Cobre NSR Royalty
description: VANCOUVER, British Columbia, June 29, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Almadex Minerals Ltd. ("Almadex" or the "Company") (TSX-V: “DEX”) is pleased to announce that it has sold the 1.75% NSR royalty on the El Cobre project located in Veracruz, Mexico to Minerales Criticos de Mexico, S.A.P.I. De C.V., a private Mexican company, for US$500,000 plus applicable taxes.

title: FuelCell Energy Secures $49 Million in EXIM Financing to Advance U.S. Clean Energy Exports

title: Martin Marietta to Combine with Lhoist North America in $13.5 Billion Transaction
description: RALEIGH, N.C., June 29, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Martin Marietta Materials, Inc. (NYSE: MLM) (Martin Marietta or the Company) today announced that it has entered into a definitive agreement to combine with Lhoist North America, Inc. (Lhoist North America or LNA), a subsidiary of Lhoist Group, for $13.5 billion in cash and shares of Martin Marietta common stock. The transaction is expected to be completed in the second half of 2026, subject to regulatory approvals.

title: Keel Infrastructure Added to Russell 3000® Index
description: NEW YORK, June 29, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Keel Infrastructure Corp. (Nasdaq: KEEL; TSX: KEEL) (“Keel Infrastructure” or “Keel”), a North American digital infrastructure and energy company, has been added as a member of the broad-market Russell 3000® Index, effective as of U.S. market open today, as part of the first 2026 Russell indexes reconstitution.

title: NATO allies promised Trump they’d secure the Arctic; they’ve got work to do
description: Through most of NATO’s eight-decade history, the inhospitable High North was low priority. That calculus is now changing.

title: 101st soldiers use drones to drop grappling hooks, breach razor wire
description: The Army should treat drones like ammo, one officer said, estimating a brigade needs between 1,000 to 1,500 drones per week in sustained combat operations.

title: Romania bolsters Black Sea fleet with Turkish-built corvette
description: The transfer marks the first delivery of a Turkish-built warship to a European Union and NATO member state, underscoring Turkey’s growing naval exports.

title: German Counter-drone startup Tytan eyes 3,000 interceptors per month in new factory
description: Production at the new plant is scheduled to begin in August, according to the company's CEO.

title: Ukraine’s top strike-drone maker moves into ballistic missile defense
description: Fire Point's drones already fly most of Kyiv's strikes inside Russia. With Germany's Hensoldt, the company is now building Freyja, a homegrown alternative.

title: Ukraine’s newest strike weapon drifts into Russia on the wind
description: The U.S. and Russia are fielding the same balloons used by Ukraine that bait air defenses and now launch missiles.

title: The Pentagon’s research infrastructure is ‘deteriorating,’ study finds
description: Pentagon research has been hampered by backlogged security clearances, limited funds to build or refurbish labs and a slow and difficult hiring process.

title: House backs $1.55 billion to revive E-7 Wedgetail, spares Navy Hawkeye
description: The White House budget office wants to raid a Navy radar-plane account and a classified Air Force line to pull the E-7 Wedgetail back from the dead.

title: Lockheed Martin wins over $35 billion contract to quadruple THAAD production
description: As U.S. lawmakers worry over the munitions stockpile, Lockheed Martin announced a multiyear contract to increase THAAD production from 96 to 400 a year.

title: Philippines deploys US-made Triton naval drones in its western waters to scout for intruders
description: The transfer comes after the military revealed that China discreetly deployed a 6x6 meter floating platform equipped with an antenna at Scarborough Shoal.

title: Europe’s defense build-up depends on getting partnerships right
description: One pattern is worth watching: Partnership activity is not spreading evenly across the market; it is coalescing around a handful of industrial platforms.

title: ICEYE to double radar-satellite capacity by late 2027 as demand surges
description: The company's goal is to ramp up production to two satellites a week as it responds to surging demand from European militaries.

title: Poland buys V-BAT drones to boost Baltic Sea naval operations
description: The deal puts Poland in line to become the first country on NATO’s eastern flank to acquire the Shield AI-made drone.

title: Trump meets munitions makers amid push to replenish weapons stockpiles
description: Pentagon negotiators are pressing contractors to move much faster to boost output.

title: White House asks Congress for $87.6 billion, mostly for Iran war
description: The request includes $67.15 billion for the military, including $21 billion for munitions and to strengthen the U.S. industrial base.

title: US, UK, France, Germany raise alarm about Chinese patrols off eastern Taiwan
description: The countries said Chinese patrols off the coast of Taiwan threaten regional stability and freedom of navigation.

title: China studied US stealth aircraft — and learned the wrong lessons
description: An Air Force researcher said China's assessments of U.S. stealth capabilities "diverge from technical reality and operational practice."

title: Airstrike killed senior ISIS commander in Syria, CENTCOM says
description: A senior Islamic State leader was killed by an airstrike last week, Central Command announced on Wednesday.

title: Gen. Christopher Donahue to unexpectedly relinquish command of Army Europe and Africa
description: U.S. Army Gen. Christopher Donahue will unexpectedly relinquish command next week after just 18 months in the role, the Pentagon confirmed Wednesday.

title: Germany scraps F126 frigate program, pivots to MEKO warships amid cost and contractor chaos
description: Germany Navy leaders have already blessed the fallback ships as capable of meeting NATO capability requirements, but an alliance deadline looms.

title: Tank maker KNDS plans stock market listing, Germany to buy 40% stake
description: The firm showed a proposed battle tank at Eurosatory defense exhibition in Paris boasting a French turret and gun mounted on a German Leopard 2 chassis.

title: US Senate joins House in voting to halt Iran war
description: It marks the first time both chambers approved directing a president to remove armed forces from hostilities since the War Powers Act was adopted in 1973.

title: US Army tests fire control software that helps moving vehicles kill drones
description: The Army has successfully tested software designed to help vehicle-mounted weapons shoot down drones while both the target and the vehicle are moving.

title: Frustrating Israel, fiber-optic killer drone technology has arrived in southern Lebanon
description: Analysts believe that any military engaged in conflict will, in one form or another, encounter technologies first seen in Ukraine.

description: The biotechnology sector’s upswing accelerated in the first half. Positive results from anticipated trials in lung cancer, Alzheimer’s and multiple autoimmune conditions could add to the momentum.

title: FDA accepts Replimune filing; Merck KGaA drops $11B on life sciences services
description: Replimune’s application is the latest example of changing attitudes at the FDA. Elsewhere, Revolution Medicines outlined more RAS-blocking data and a Definium stock offering brought in $800 million.

title: At BIO, tension over China biotech deal regulation looms
description: Biotech investors, executives and analysts worry that blanket bans on deals with Chinese companies could curb domestic entrepreneurs’ ambitions in the sector.

title: Alkermes’ incoming CEO has a plan to outplay Lilly and Takeda
description: Blair Jackson says the next 12 months may be the most important in Alkermes’ history, as the brain drugmaker races to join an “orexin” market that analysts see as a multibillion-dollar opportunity.

title: On second try, Acadia drug gets nod from European regulators
description: The influential CHMP committee also threw its support behind more than a dozen other medicines while recommending the marketing authorization for Tavneos be revoked.

title: Remix to go public in reverse merger with once high-flying Passage Bio
description: The transaction marks the latest turn in the fortunes of Passage Bio, one of the companies started by gene therapy pioneer Jim Wilson.

title: Antares gets $105M from Novartis for new cancer drugs
description: Adam Friedman, CEO of the Scorpion Therapeutics spinout, said the deal helps both companies accelerate research "faster than either of us could alone."

title: Ionis, with new FDA nod, hits a ‘commercial inflection point’
description: Historically seen as a "platform monetization story," according to one analyst, Ionis now has a potential multibillion-dollar medicine in Tryngolza, which secured expanded approval Wednesday.

title: Investors pour $115M into RQ Bio in search of a preventive flu drug
description: The startup claims to have a “differentiated” antibody that can provide long-lasting protection against a variety of influenza strains.

title: To Wall Street, psychedelics biotechs are more teammates than rivals
description: Some analysts argue that, even for competitors, a big clinical trial win for Definium Therapeutics helps validate the psychedelic field and “bring it even further into the mainstream.”
